message:
feat(runtime_math): explicit SafetyLevel parameter for deterministic testing

Remove reliance on global environment-backed config during tests and
harness scenarios by threading SafetyLevel explicitly through the
RuntimeMathKernel API.

Key changes:
- Add new_for_mode(SafetyLevel) constructor for explicit mode control
- Update observe_validation_result() to accept mode parameter
- Update note_check_order_outcome() to accept mode parameter
- Add runtime_math_determinism_proofs.rs for formal verification

This enables reproducible test execution independent of environment
variables, critical for CI determinism and property-based testing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1es","title":"RaptorQ Runtime: Deterministic repair symbol generation (overhead model + R derivation)","description":"Implement or specify deterministic repair symbol generation for evidence objects.

Spec-derived formula to adopt:
- slack_decode = 2 (target K+2 decode slack)
- R = max(slack_decode, ceil(K_source * overhead_percent / 100))
- loss_fraction_max approx (R - slack_decode)/(K_source + R)

Constraints:
- Generation MUST NOT run per call; run on epoch boundary/cadence.
- Seed must be deterministic from epoch/object id.

Deliverables:
- A minimal encoder interface and test vectors.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GrayPond","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:34:56.148303508Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T01:34:50.848397590Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T01:34:28.850099524Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1es","depends_on_id":"bd-3a9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:35:09.818784628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1es","depends_on_id":"bd-kom","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:35:09.739481467Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":46,"issue_id":"bd-1es","author":"FuchsiaHollow","text":"Verified complete. All deliverables present in evidence.rs. 9/9 tests pass.","created_at":"2026-02-11T01:34:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1f35","title":"bd-yos subtask: Deterministic thread stress suite (strict+hardened)","description":"Background:
- Threading bugs often appear only under sustained stress and mixed operation patterns.

Goal:
- Build deterministic threading stress suite covering create/join/detach under strict+hardened modes.

Deliverables:
1) Stress scenario set (fan-out/fan-in, churn, mixed detach/join).
2) Deterministic replay controls and failure triage artifacts.
3) Integration into e2e gate sequence.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Stress suite passes reproducibly on CI-capable environment.
- Failure cases produce actionable logs and bounded reproduction recipes.

Verification & Logging:
- E2E scripts with explicit seed/timeouts.
- Structured logs: trace_id, scenario_id, mode, op_counts, failure_marker, timing, artifact_refs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:00:52.224033729Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:00:52.224033729Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","e2e","logging","pthread","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-1ff3","title":"bd-25n subtask: Hard-parts unit packs (startup/NSS/iconv/setjmp)","description":"Background:
- Hard-parts subsystems (startup/NSS/iconv/setjmp) need consistent unit-test depth and diagnostics.

Goal:
- Expand unit packs across startup, NSS, iconv, and control-transfer subsystems.

Deliverables:
1) Subsystem-specific positive/negative/adversarial test matrices.
2) Reproducible fixture helpers and deterministic seeds.
3) Standardized assertion/report structure for cross-subsystem debugging.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Each hard-parts subsystem has comprehensive unit coverage in declared scope.
- Failures identify subsystem, contract clause, and evidence path.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit suites per subsystem.
- Structured logs for subsystem test events and artifacts.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:02:39.096255239Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:02:39.096255239Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","hard-parts","testing","unit","verification"]}
-{"id":"bd-1fk1","title":"bd-25n subtask: Runtime-math determinism and invariant regression suite","description":"Background:
- Runtime-math complexity requires strong regression guards for deterministic behavior and invariants.

Goal:
- Expand runtime-math controller test depth (determinism, invariants, state transitions, snapshot consistency).

Deliverables:
1) Determinism tests for decide/observe under fixed inputs.
2) Invariant tests for controller-specific bounds and state transitions.
3) Snapshot schema/field consistency tests for active production set.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Controller regressions are caught before integration.
- Snapshot consistency remains stable under additive changes policy.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests plus targeted integration tests.
- Structured logs with controller_id, state_before, state_after, invariant_result.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:02:39.198307074Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:02:39.198307074Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","math","testing","unit","verification"]}
+{"id":"bd-1fk1","title":"bd-25n subtask: Runtime-math determinism and invariant regression suite","description":"Background:
- Runtime-math complexity requires strong regression guards for deterministic behavior and invariants.

Goal:
- Expand runtime-math controller test depth (determinism, invariants, state transitions, snapshot consistency).

Deliverables:
1) Determinism tests for decide/observe under fixed inputs.
2) Invariant tests for controller-specific bounds and state transitions.
3) Snapshot schema/field consistency tests for active production set.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Controller regressions are caught before integration.
- Snapshot consistency remains stable under additive changes policy.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests plus targeted integration tests.
- Structured logs with controller_id, state_before, state_after, invariant_result.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilverLake","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:02:39.198307074Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T23:40:46.730177037Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","math","testing","unit","verification"],"comments":[{"id":122,"issue_id":"bd-1fk1","author":"SilverLake","text":"Execution plan (bd-1fk1):

Goal: deterministic + invariant regression suite for runtime_math integration (decide + observe_validation_result), independent of golden fixture updates.

Planned deliverables:
1) Add a constructor that avoids env coupling in tests: RuntimeMathKernel::new_for_mode(mode: SafetyLevel) used by determinism tests (RuntimeMathKernel::new() remains env-backed).
2) Add a harness-side deterministic scenario that exercises BOTH decide() and observe_validation_result() with fixed inputs and asserts:
   - per-step RuntimeDecision equality across two fresh kernels
   - final RuntimeKernelSnapshot equality across two fresh kernels
   - evidence seqno monotonicity + cadence/adverse-record determinism (no sampling drift)
3) Add invariant checks over snapshots (range/finite/monotone constraints) with structured JSONL logs per check:
   - controller_id (or invariant_id)
   - state_before snapshot
   - state_after snapshot
   - invariant_result + failures
4) Add a schema-consistency guard: snapshot schema version constant matches schema documentation and stays additive-only.

Local env note: /tmp is full on this machine; avoid heredoc-based scripts while implementing; use cargo tests and TMPDIR=$PWD/.tmp where needed.","created_at":"2026-02-12T23:40:46Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1gg","title":"EPIC: Multi-Architecture Support (aarch64)","description":"Goal: Expand architecture support beyond x86_64.

Current State:
- x86_64: Primary target, raw syscall veneer implemented
- aarch64: Not implemented
- i386: Not implemented
- Others: Not planned initially

Architecture Expansion Strategy:
1. aarch64 first (modern, clean ABI, high value)
2. Common syscall abstraction layer
3. Per-arch assembly veneers (minimal)
4. Per-arch ABI quirks (va_list, TLS, etc.)
5. CI matrix for multi-arch testing

aarch64 Requirements:
- Raw syscall veneer (different syscall numbers, registers)
- TLS handling (TPIDR_EL0)
- Signal frame layout
- setjmp/longjmp register sets
- SIMD (NEON) considerations for string ops

Testing:
- QEMU user-mode for CI
- Real hardware validation
- Cross-compile + test workflow

Success Criteria:
- aarch64 passes same conformance suite as x86_64
- Performance within 2x of x86_64 (accounting for emulation)
- No architecture-specific memory safety regressions","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:58:23.976629579Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:36.772402982Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["arch","multiplatform"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1gg","depends_on_id":"bd-2vv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:36.661828262Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1gg","depends_on_id":"bd-h5x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:36.772359831Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1gg.1","title":"aarch64: raw syscall veneer","description":"aarch64 raw syscall veneer implementation.

Goal: Implement raw Linux syscall interface for aarch64.

Technical Details:
aarch64 syscall convention:
- Syscall number: x8
- Arguments: x0, x1, x2, x3, x4, x5
- Return value: x0
- Error: negative return value
- Clobbered: none (callee-save ABI)

Syscall Numbers:
aarch64 has different syscall numbers than x86_64.
Reference: /usr/include/asm-generic/unistd.h

Key Differences from x86_64:
- No 32-bit compat syscalls
- Different TLS mechanism (TPIDR_EL0)
- Different signal frame layout
- Different function calling convention

Implementation:
1. syscall_veneer.rs for aarch64 target
2. Conditional compilation with cfg(target_arch)
3. Same safe Rust interface as x86_64
4. Inline assembly for syscall instruction

Testing:
- Cross-compile to aarch64
- Test under QEMU user mode
- Compare syscall behavior with x86_64
- Verify error handling

Success Criteria:
- All syscalls from x86_64 veneer available on aarch64
- Tests pass under QEMU
- Same safe Rust interface","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:02:49.782622009Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:02:49.782622009Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aarch64","arch","syscall"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1gg.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1gg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:02:49.782622009Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1gg.2","title":"aarch64: TLS implementation","description":"aarch64 TLS (Thread-Local Storage) implementation.

Goal: Implement TLS mechanism for aarch64.

Technical Details:
aarch64 TLS uses TPIDR_EL0 register:
- Read: mrs x0, tpidr_el0
- Write: msr tpidr_el0, x0

TLS Layout:
- Different from x86_64 (fs: segment)
- Thread pointer points to TCB (Thread Control Block)
- Static TLS follows TCB
- Dynamic TLS uses dtv (Dynamic Thread Vector)

Implementation:
1. Thread pointer read/write primitives
2. TLS initialization during thread creation
3. Static TLS allocation
4. __tls_get_addr for dynamic TLS

Interactions:
- pthread_create must set up TLS
- TLS variables (__thread) must work
- Membrane TLS cache must work

Testing:
- __thread variable access
- pthread_getspecific/setspecific
- TLS across thread boundaries
- Membrane TLS cache correctness

Success Criteria:
- __thread variables work correctly
- pthread_key_* operations work
- Membrane TLS cache functional on aarch64","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:02:55.083406560Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:36.882580147Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aarch64","arch","tls"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1gg.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1gg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:02:55.083406560Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1gg.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1gg.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:36.882532558Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
